# Delivery Report

## Final Artifact

toktra Chrome MV3 extension with the five-layer realtime translation pipeline implemented in `extension/`.

## How To Run Locally

1. Open `chrome://extensions/`.
2. Enable Developer Mode.
3. Load unpacked extension from:

```text
tools/toktra/extension
```

## What Was Delivered

- Segmenter metadata and site-rule parsing in `extension/core.js`.
- Scheduler planning and progress rendering in `extension/core.js` and `extension/content.js`.
- Three-screen lazy loading for normal web pages and PDF pages.
- ID-keyed Provider layer and namespaced cache in `extension/background.js`.
- Text-layer PDF translation viewer using bundled pdf.js files.
- Site-rule settings in `extension/options.html` and `extension/options.js`.
- Tests and longtask docs.

## Hard Gate Status

| Gate | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Tests | Pass | `npm test`: 75 tests, 0 failures |
| Lint | Pass | `npm run lint`: manifest and 18 extension files checked |
| Local deploy | Ready | Unpacked extension path above |

## Final Score

| Dimension | Score | Evidence |
|---|---:|---|
| Five-layer architecture | 92 | `docs/EvalReport.md` |

## Unresolved Failures Or Risks

- Live Chrome smoke with real API key was not run in this environment.
- PDF support is text-layer based, not a pixel-perfect PDF renderer.
- Only OpenAI-compatible provider is implemented; Chrome Translator/local providers remain next-step additions.

## Handoff Notes

Reload the unpacked extension after these changes. Existing v1 cache remains stored but the new namespaced v2 cache will be used for new translation calls.

## Next Recommended Iteration

- Add Chrome built-in Translator API provider as a no-key/local-first option.
- Add optional auto-hide delay for complete progress UI.
- Add browser smoke automation for TDWH and Wikipedia pages.
